questions.question: What is the name of the petitioner in the landmark SC judgement on basic structure of the Constitution, who has recently passed away?
questions.explanation: Kesavananda Bharati, who was a petitioner of the landmark Supreme Court judgement on basic structure of the Constitution in 1973, has recently passed away.He was the head of a Mutt at Kasaragod in Kerala, and had moved the Supreme Court challenging the Kerala government’s land reforms Act of 1969. Supreme Court ruled that the basic structure of the Constitution is unbreakable and cannot be amended by Parliament.

questions.question: The first batch of Rafael Fighter Jets are to be inducted into which squadron of IAF?
questions.explanation: The formal ceremony to induct 5 Rafale fighter aircrafts to Indian Air Force is scheduled to be held at Ambala airbase in Haryana on September 10.The aircrafts are to be inducted into the No.17 Squadron of IAF also known as “Golden Arrows”. French Defence Minister Florence Parly is expected to take part in the induction ceremony.

questions.question: India has partnered with Israel and which other country 5G communication network?
questions.explanation: India, Israel and the United States of America have begun collaboration in developmental areas. These include development of a transparent, open, reliable and secure 5G communication network. This collaboration among the three countries is a result of people-to-people collaboration, particularly by Indian Diaspora in the US and Israel.

questions.question: What is the new name of Indian Civil & Administrative Association?
questions.explanation: The Indian Civil & Administrative Services (Central) Association has been renamed as “IAS Association” recently. The new name was unanimously cleared in the body’s Annual General Meeting there by dropping the reference to the ‘colonial-era’ ICS. Also, a new moto “Yogah Karmasu Kaushalam” meaning “Perfection in action is Yogah” has been chosen during the meeting.

questions.question: The CCEA has approved monetization of assets of which PSU through InvIT model?
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) chaired by the PM by, has approved monetization of assets of Power Grid. Power Grid is a Public Sector Undertaking under Ministry of Power. The monetization is being done for the first time through Infrastructure Investment Trust (InvIT) model. The proceeds of asset monetization would be used to fund new and on-going projects of the PSU.

questions.question: Which institution has directed for appointment of woman IPS officer in every district of Kerala to oversee POCSO related cases?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Women and Child Development

questions.explanation: The High Court of Kerala has issued a set of directives to the state regarding Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act (POCSO). The High Court has directed the state to appoint woman IPS officer in every district, to oversee the cases investigated under POSCO Act. It has also directed to establish One-Stop Support Centres, in lines with the Supreme Court directions, to protect the victims.

questions.question: Which country has revoked the Visas of more than 1,000 Chinese students recently?
questions.explanation: The United States of America (USA) has recently revoked the Visas of more than 1,000 Chinese students and researchers. The move is under the directions of the US President Donald Trump in May 2020. The proclamation is aimed at Chinese nationals who are suspected to have ties with Chinese military. In this regard, China has accused US of racial discrimination.

questions.question: Who is the head of Empowered Group of Ministers constituted by the Government to oversee the corporatization of Ordnance Factory Board?
questions.explanation: The Government of India has set up an Empowered Group of Ministers headed by the Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, to oversee and guide the entire process of corporatization of Ordnance Factory Board.The Union Government recently announced that Ordnance Factory Board (OFB) – a subordinate office of Defence Ministry would be converted into one or more 100 % Government owned corporate entities.

questions.question: As per a recent study, which Vitamin supplement could decrease Covid-19 admission rate in ICU?
questions.explanation: A new study published in the Journal of Steroid Biochemistry and Molecular Biology has found that Vitamin D supplements can decrease the rate of ICU admissions due to Covid-19.The vitamin is found to be stimulating immune responses against various diseases including the novel coronavirus. The study was conducted on 76 patients who tested positive for Covid-19 in Spain. The patients who received the supplement were discharged without complications.

questions.question: Post Brexit, UK signed its first economic partnership agreement with which country?
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom (UK) has entered into an Economic Partnership Agreement with Japan. This partnership is seen as a historic move, since this is the first trade agreement signed by the UK post Brexit. The agreement is said to foster trade between the two countries by about 15 billion Euros.
